task_completion.description: Log in to FreeMED using admin credentials
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeMED dashboard or interface indicating the user is logged in as 'admin'.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Place of Service (POS) billing dictionary
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeMED interface navigated to the Billing Dictionary or Place of Service codes section.
Screenshots show '03' typed into the POS Code input field in the new record form.
Screenshots show 'School' typed into the Name input field in the new record form.
Screenshots show 'A facility whose primary purpose is education.' typed into the Description input field.

task_completion.description: Save the new POS code record
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a success message indicating the record was saved, or the new POS code '03' ('School') is visible in the list of existing Place of Service codes.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to interact with FreeMED
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser with the FreeMED interface, rather than terminal commands or API testing tools.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine FreeMED application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches a real FreeMED installation, not a local text editor, mock HTML page, or notes app.

integrity.description: Agent filled out the form fields in the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots capture the intermediate steps of the form being filled out, proving the agent didn't bypass the UI via direct database insertion scripts.

privileged_info_for_vlm: According to the Official CMS Place of Service Code Set, POS Code '03' corresponds to 'School' with the description 'A facility whose primary purpose is education. (Effective January 1, 2003)'. The VLM should verify that the code '03', name 'School', and the description (either the exact task string 'A facility whose primary purpose is education.' or the full CMS string) are successfully entered into the FreeMED system.
